commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the agent read-tool wrapper to scale the default read byte cap from the active model context window, preserve explicit caller limits, and add regression coverage for capped pagination edge cases.

PR surface: Source +137, Tests +184. Total +321 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction of the broader Windows stall is available here. Source review does show current main has a 32KB adaptive read floor and the PR exercises the smaller-cap path with focused tests.
commands.review_followup: - Human maintainer review is needed for the protected maintainer label and the compatibility-sensitive implicit default reduction; no automated repair is indicated.

target.title: fix(agents): scale read output for small contexts
target.body:
This keeps default `read` output smaller for small-context local models without changing explicit caller limits. It is part of the small-model stability work: smaller models were getting oversized file reads by default, then losing useful context or stalling around follow-up tool use.

Refs #86599.

## Summary

- Scale the adaptive default `read` byte cap from the active model context window, with an 8KB floor and 128KB ceiling.
- Preserve explicit `limit` values, including `0` and negative values that the base read tool normalizes to one line.
- Keep continuation guidance inside the byte budget and avoid misleading offsets when the first line or trailing whitespace-only lines hit the cap.
- Add focused coverage for small-context caps, long lines, explicit limits, whitespace-only capped content, EOF pagination, and continuation-text-in-file false positives.

## Verification

Latest pushed head: `d62ff11f834` on base `94b1427fdfa`.

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed after rebase.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts` passed after rebase.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts` passed after rebase.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed after rebase: 1 Vitest shard, 15 tests.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed clean after rebase: no accepted/actionable findings, overall 0.84.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed before the final rebase with exit 0: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4b41ba6b3f30`, run `run_4d8458e94046`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, lease stopped.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` was rerun after rebasing onto `94b1427fdfa`; the touched core/typecheck lanes passed, then the gate failed during `lint core changed files` while preparing Discord boundary DTS for an unrelated current-main error in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts:165`. Run: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_f850f1316358`, run `run_d1a10af9276d`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, lease stopped. The failure is from current main commit `94b1427fdfa` and is not in this PR's diff.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: small-context models get a smaller default file-read payload, while explicit caller limits keep the existing read contract.

Real environment tested: local focused node-wrapper tests for read-tool behavior; AWS Crabbox Linux changed-gate runner.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused read-tool tests passed on pushed SHA `d62ff11f834`; AWS Crabbox `run_4d8458e94046` completed with exit 0 before the final rebase and covered the `core`/`coreTests` lanes; post-rebase AWS Crabbox `run_d1a10af9276d` reached the unrelated current-main Discord boundary DTS failure after core typecheck and core-test typecheck passed.

Observed result after fix: adaptive reads cap output for small context windows, explicit limits still delegate to the base read tool, capped whitespace-only pages keep emitted content aligned with continuation offsets, and continuation-looking file text is not treated as pagination metadata.

What was not tested: real Ollama/model-provider live chat; this PR changes read-tool context sizing, not provider streaming.
